reasoning:
The resolution depends on whether fatalities in the 30 days prior to each date exceed the 360-day historical average of ~44.5 per month. 
Conflict fatalities in Chad are highly right-skewed. Taking the 44.5 per month average (which equals ~535 deaths per year), almost all of these fatalities in the past year can be attributed to a few massive spikes in the conflict against Boko Haram in the Lake Chad region: ~40 deaths in Oct 2024, ~112 in Nov 2024, and ~327 in a major offensive that the army announced had concluded on February 18, 2025. This means that "normal" baseline fatalities across the rest of the year are extremely low (<10 deaths per month).

1. **2025-03-23**: The 30-day window for this resolution date is Feb 21 – Mar 23. This perfectly misses the massive death toll from the Boko Haram offensive that officially concluded on Feb 18. Exhaustive searches show no major violent events have been reported since Feb 21, and only 7 days remain in the window. The probability of hitting 45+ deaths in the final week without any ongoing major operation is very low (8%).
2. **Further Dates**: Without an ongoing offensive, random 30-day periods are highly likely to fall below the inflated average of 44.5. Historically, only 2-3 months a year experience massive death tolls that exceed this threshold. As such, the base rate for an arbitrary future month to exceed 44.5 fatalities is roughly 20-30%. 
Following instructions to increase uncertainty (pulling predictions toward 0.5) over longer time horizons, the probabilities scale up gradually for the subsequent resolution dates from 18% to 36%.
